# booklife-mcp

> MCP server for managing reading lists across Hardcover, Libby, and local TBR -- a unified AI-powered reading assistant.

## What It Does

BookLife bridges Hardcover (reading tracker), Libby/OverDrive (library access), and Open Library (metadata) into a single MCP server. Discover books, borrow from the library, track reading progress, manage a unified TBR list, sync across services, and get content-based recommendations -- all through natural conversation.

## Install

```bash
# Build from source (Go 1.23+)
git clone https://github.com/bbdaniels/booklife-mcp.git
cd booklife-mcp/booklife-mcp && make install

# Add to Claude Code
claude mcp add booklife -e HARDCOVER_API_KEY=your-key -- ~/.local/bin/booklife serve
```

## Tools

- **info** -- Progressive discovery: list tools, workflows, and categories
- **sync** -- Universal sync: Libby history -> Hardcover "read" status -> enrichment -> tag cache
- **profile_get** -- Reading profile with format preferences, genres, cadence, streaks

### Hardcover
- **hardcover_get_my_library** -- Get reading list (reading, want-to-read, read) with pagination
- **hardcover_add_to_library** -- Add a book by ISBN or title/author
- **hardcover_update_reading_status** -- Update status, progress, or rating

### TBR (Unified To-Be-Read)
- **tbr_list** -- List unified TBR across all sources (physical, hardcover, libby)
- **tbr_add** -- Add a book manually (physical books or custom entries)
- **tbr_remove** -- Remove a book from TBR
- **tbr_search** -- Search TBR by title or author
- **tbr_stats** -- TBR breakdown by source and availability
- **tbr_sync** -- Sync TBR from Hardcover, Libby holds, or Libby tags

### Search & Access
- **booklife_find_book_everywhere** -- Search all sources for a book with availability info
- **booklife_best_way_to_read** -- Determine best access method (library, bookstore, etc.)

### History & Analytics
- **history_get** -- Browse reading history with pagination and search
- **history_stats** -- Reading statistics by format, library, year
- **history_import_timeline** -- Import Libby timeline from export URL
- **history_sync_current_loans** -- Capture current Libby loans for tracking

### Enrichment & Recommendations
- **enrichment_enrich_history** -- Background job to fetch metadata from Open Library/Google Books
- **enrichment_status** -- Monitor enrichment job progress
- **book_find_similar** -- Content-based recommendations using enriched metadata

## Configuration

Required environment variable:
- `HARDCOVER_API_KEY` -- Get from https://hardcover.app/settings/api

Optional:
- `BOOKLIFE_DATA_DIR` -- Data directory (default: `~/.local/share/booklife`)
- `BOOKLIFE_LOG_LEVEL` -- `debug` / `info` / `warn` / `error`

Libby auth: Run `booklife libby-connect <8-digit-code>` (get code from Libby app: Settings -> Copy To Another Device -> Sonos Speakers).

Config file: `~/.config/booklife/booklife.kdl` (see README for full KDL schema).
